mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS currently at 0.37% indicating deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment of more than 100,000 persons signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 453,438 million dollars, HOUST at 1,089 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production index above 104.0 (2017=100) signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 104.0 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rise above 5.5% signals labor market slack that increases recession probability and reduces equity risk appetite. This threshold crossing triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk elevation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand that supports equity earnings growth. When both series show positive quarter-over-quarter growth simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn positive over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both grow > 0.5% in 1 quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,492.7 billion chained 2017 dollars, RSAFS at 453,438 million dollars
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion or mid-cycle acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained inflation signal (CPIAUCSL rising > 3% year-over-year) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) indicates restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction effect (inflation + high rates) shows no incremental predictive power beyond single-series models (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,000 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration or housing market contraction
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 1,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
